checks.a_analysis: I have carefully reviewed the output JSON against the source document. All text elements in the output, including headings ('NC Parcels', 'NC Parcels Project Status by County', 'Thank you!'), dates, list items from the legend, and source attribution, are directly derived from the source images. No fabricated paragraphs, sentences, or section headings were found. The metadata title 'Logo Imagery Graphic Colors Map Imagery 6E2E' is an allowed AI-generated addition for accessibility.

I verified the contact emails on page 3 of the source document against the output JSON. The emails 'katie.doherty@rutherfordcountync.gov', 'relkins@sosnc.gov', and 'elizabeth.daniel@nc.gov' appear exactly as they do in the source. There are no other contact details such as phone numbers or URLs to verify. No fabrication occurred.

The source document consists of 3 pages. The output JSON successfully captured content from all three pages: the title slide (Page 1), the map slide with legend and sources (Page 2), and the contact slide (Page 3). No whole sections or pages were omitted during conversion.
There are no tables present in the source document. The legend data on page 2 has been converted into a list format, which is an acceptable representation of that structured data.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: I cross-checked the text content of each page. Page 1 title and date are present. Page 2 map title, date, full legend list (including the numeric values in parentheses), and full source attribution paragraph are present. Page 3 'Thank you!' and all contact emails are present. There are no significant text omissions within any section.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text in the output JSON matches the source document's informational content exactly. There are no changes to names, dates, email addresses, or map legend data that would alter the meaning. The structural conversion of the legend into a list is a formatting choice that preserves informational fidelity.

checks.a_analysis: Each text block and content element in the output corresponds to a unique element in the source document. While the date 'October 7, 2025' appears twice in the JSON, it is intentionally repeated in the source document on both the title page (page 1) and the map page (page 2). There are no instances of erroneous duplication.

The heading hierarchy is correctly implemented. Page 1 uses an H1 for the main title. Page 2 uses an H1 for the map title and an H2 for the date, creating a logical parent-child relationship. Page 3 uses an H1 for the closing statement. This structure is consistent and accessible for screen readers.
The source document does not contain any tables. The output correctly reflects this absence by using lists and paragraphs for descriptive content.

The reading order follows the logical top-to-bottom, left-to-right flow of the pages. On page 2, the descriptive list (legend) and source text are placed before the map image in the JSON sequence; this is a standard accessibility optimization providing context before a complex graphic and does not constitute a defect.

The map legend on page 2 is correctly identified and converted into an unordered list. The list items accurately reflect the entries in the PDF legend, including counts and categories. The meaning and structure of the list are preserved.

The contact section on page 3 contains three distinct government email addresses. In the output JSON, these are rendered as plain text within a paragraph rather than as functional link elements. Per the instructions, visible contact links in the PDF that are not clickable/linked in the output are considered missing links.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML version accurately preserves the visual organization and flow of the source document. The transition from the PDF's split-pane cover page to a linear HTML layout is a standard responsive conversion that maintains the logical order of information. The content from each page is clearly demarcated into sections with appropriate headings. The addition of a navigation area and table of contents is an expected enhancement for accessibility and does not detract from the visual fidelity.
The source document does not contain any tables; therefore, no table rendering issues are present in the HTML output.

All meaningful visual elements from the PDF are present in the HTML. The primary NC DIT logo is featured at the top, and the complex map graphic from page 2 is successfully rendered as an image including its internal components (north arrow, NCGICC logo, legend). The absence of repeated branding logos (NC DIT) at the bottom of every section is a standard optimization for single-page web layouts and does not represent a loss of meaningful content.

No genuine body content duplication was observed. While textual information such as the 'Sources' and 'Parcel Updates' legend appear both as HTML text and within the map graphic, this is an intentional accessibility feature providing a machine-readable version of the graphic's data. Headlines correctly appear in both the navigation/TOC area and the main document body as intended for accessible navigation.
